codeReview.summary: The follow-up fix does resolve the original non-array JSON duplicate-provenance bug in `session_input_callback` by assigning per-item provenance before callback execution and covering object/number/bool/null regressions. However, it introduces that provenance by adding a new field to the public `InputItem::{Text, Json}` enum variants, which is a source-breaking API change for downstream callers and therefore a blocking regression.

issues.description: `InputItem::Text` and `InputItem::Json` now carry a public `provenance` field (`crates/agents-core/src/items.rs:13-24`). Because `InputItem` is re-exported from the public facade (`crates/openai-agents/src/lib.rs:17`), this changes the construction and pattern-match shape seen by downstream crates: existing code written as `InputItem::Json { value }` or `InputItem::Text { text }` no longer compiles unless it is updated to add `..` or `provenance: None`. The feature only needed internal callback provenance tracking, so introducing an internal bookkeeping field into the public enum layout is a breaking API regression.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission guidance does not explicitly warn that doc-hidden fields on public Rust enum variants are still source-breaking API changes. This fix used that pattern for internal provenance bookkeeping and ended up touching 17 files across the workspace to adapt constructors and pattern matches.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `crates/agents-core/src/items.rs:13-24` adds the hidden `provenance` field to public `InputItem` variants; `crates/openai-agents/src/lib.rs:17` re-exports `InputItem`; commit `895615bdda78052fecb63909d6134d990ca5f66d` changes 17 files, many only to thread `provenance: None` or `..` through public constructors/pattern matches.

summary: Reviewed the original failed duplicate-provenance fix, the rerun fix commit, both handoffs, the fix transcript skeleton, and the shared-state guidance. The rerun correctly addresses the prior non-array JSON attribution gap in `session_input_callback`, but the implementation fails scrutiny because it achieves that by changing the public `InputItem` enum variant shape, creating a new downstream-breaking API regression.
